mechanism: Linear regression with robustness checks. Estimate how individual choices over binary justice principles (accountability, libertarian, egalitarian, bias) vary with treatment conditions. Iteratively apply least-squares minimization, median regression, M-estimators, and hurdle models to account for outliers and the spike at zero. Test null hypotheses on parameter vectors.

Between-subjects and within-subjects factorial designs vary judgment-violation and resolution-violation independently. Collect ordinal ratings on Likert scales. Compute descriptive statistics (percentages agreeing, mean responses). Apply ANOVA and t-tests to detect main effects and interactions. Prototype theory explains cluster of overlapping features rather than classical sufficient/necessary conditions.

Boundary-shift eye-tracking paradigm: reader fixates sequence of words; invisible boundary triggers display change during saccade. Measure first fixation, gaze duration, single-fixation duration, skipping probability across conditions. Linear mixed models with participants and items as random effects. Compare identical vs dissimilar preview conditions at different boundary locations.

Regression of health outcomes (antenatal care, clinic delivery, trained attendant, child vaccines) on estimated/imputed regional HIV prevalence over time. Two strategies: (1) linear assumption on prevalence growth from 1980; (2) flexible year-by-prevalence interactions. Include region fixed effects and birth-year effects. Semi-parametric graphical analysis relaxes linearity; test whether divergence in health services begins at break-point in mid-1990s.

Linear fixed-effects panel model removes time-invariant unobserved heterogeneity. Estimate causal effect of partnership state transitions on ordinal well-being scores (0–10 scale). Include lagged happiness to test reverse causality. Stratify by age cohort and sexual orientation; test symmetry of formation and dissolution effects via paired hypothesis tests.

Factorial within-subject designs vary omission vs commission while holding intentions and outcomes constant. Subjects rate moral judgments on 0 to -100 scales. Ordinal comparisons and binomial tests detect whether commission is rated worse. Collect written justifications; code causal-attribution reasoning. Test hypotheses about movement, detectability, alternative causes, presence, repeatability, diffusion of responsibility.
